summary: free-anthropic-claude is a brand-impersonation dropper that fetches and executes a Python crypto-wallet stealer at install
details: Package published under the name `free-anthropic-claude` with description 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper' but its README and code claim to be an unrelated cachesync-helper. The actual behavior is a multi-platform malware dropper. The postinstall hook (`node lib/cli.js`) calls `createCache()` which triggers warmup/prefetch routines in `lib/index.js` that POST to four hardcoded bare IPs (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146:8443) with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized:false`), receive base64-encoded payload files, and write them under deliberately disguised paths chosen to mimic legitimate OS components: `~/.local/share/com.apple.sync` (macOS), `~/.local/share/prometheus` (Linux), and `AppData/Local/Microsoft/Windows Security/Health` (Windows). The dropped Python `main.py` is then spawned detached. If no Python interpreter is present, the dropper silently bootstraps one — running `winget install Python.Python.3.12`, `brew install python3`, or downloading Miniconda from repo.anaconda.com and installing it into a hidden directory — then pip-installs crypto-wallet libraries (`bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `eth-account`, `psutil`), revealing the payload purpose as cryptocurrency wallet/seed-phrase theft. On macOS, the dropper directly writes ALLOW entries into the user's TCC privacy database (`~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db`) via sqlite3 for `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData`, granting Terminal, Node, and `/usr/bin/python3` Full Disk Access without user consent — a direct attack on the OS privacy/consent layer. All sensitive identifiers throughout `lib/index.js` are split-and-joined to evade scanners (`['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['exec','File','Sync'].join('')`, `['ba','se','64'].join('')`, `module[['constr','uctor'].join('')]['_l'+'oad']`, etc.). This is unambiguous, multi-vector malware: brand-impersonation lure + bare-IP C2 + alternate-runtime bootstrap + macOS TCC privilege escalation + filesystem persistence under disguised system paths.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
